title: Cotoneaster integrifolius
intro: Cotoneaster integrifolius, also known as the Tibetan cotoneaster, is a shrub native to the mountainous regions of the Himalayas and Tibet, belonging to the Rosaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which provide relief from pain and promote overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Cotoneaster integrifolius include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional Tibetan medicine, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, and it is also sometimes used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic properties.
taxonomy: Cotoneaster integrifolius is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is native to China and is often found growing in mountainous regions. The plant is a deciduous shrub that typically grows to a height of 3-6 meters. The classification of Cotoneaster integrifolius is further divided into the binomial nomenclature, where the genus is Cotoneaster and the species is integrifolius. This classification helps in identifying and distinguishing the plant from other similar species within the Rosaceae family.
